prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register-customer_order source_fulfillment_unit_a)
(register-customer_order destination_fulfillment_unit_a)
(register-customer_order shipment_a)
(register-customer_order shipment_b)
(assign-source_location source_fulfillment_unit_a warehouse_a)
(attach-release_window source_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(authorize-processing_release source_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(allocate-handling_resource source_fulfillment_unit_a pack_station_a)
(open-source_segment source_fulfillment_unit_a source_shipment_segment_a cutoff_window_a)
(activate-source_unit source_fulfillment_unit_a source_shipment_segment_a pack_station_a)
(free-handling_resource source_fulfillment_unit_a pack_station_a)
(reopen-release_window source_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(assign-source_location destination_fulfillment_unit_a warehouse_b)
(attach-release_window destination_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(authorize-processing_release destination_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(allocate-handling_resource destination_fulfillment_unit_a pack_station_a)
(open-destination_segment destination_fulfillment_unit_a destination_shipment_segment_a cutoff_window_a)
(activate-destination_unit destination_fulfillment_unit_a destination_shipment_segment_a pack_station_a)
(free-handling_resource destination_fulfillment_unit_a pack_station_a)
(reopen-release_window destination_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(reserve-transport_unit_for_segments source_fulfillment_unit_a destination_fulfillment_unit_a source_shipment_segment_a destination_shipment_segment_a transport_unit_a)
(attach-release_window source_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(stage-transport_unit transport_unit_a source_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(release-source_fulfillment_unit source_fulfillment_unit_a transport_unit_a)
(release-destination_fulfillment_unit destination_fulfillment_unit_a transport_unit_a)
(commit-outbound_release_with_service_level source_fulfillment_unit_a express_service_level cutoff_window_a)
(close-source_fulfillment_unit source_fulfillment_unit_a warehouse_a express_service_level)
(reopen-release_window source_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(attach-release_window destination_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(commit-outbound_release_with_service_level destination_fulfillment_unit_a express_service_level cutoff_window_a)
(close-destination_fulfillment_unit destination_fulfillment_unit_a warehouse_b express_service_level)
(reopen-release_window destination_fulfillment_unit_a cutoff_window_a)
(assign-source_location shipment_a warehouse_c)
(attach-release_window shipment_a cutoff_window_a)
(authorize-processing_release shipment_a cutoff_window_a)
(attach-temperature_profile shipment_a cold_chain_profile)
(select-carrier_preference shipment_a carrier_preference_a cutoff_window_a)
(allocate-handling_resource shipment_a pack_station_a)
(prepare-carrier_booking shipment_a pack_station_a)
(confirm-carrier_manifest shipment_a cold_chain_profile)
(commit-dispatch_clearance shipment_a)
(commit-outbound_release_with_service_level shipment_a express_service_level cutoff_window_a)
(close-shipment_outbound shipment_a warehouse_c express_service_level)
(free-handling_resource shipment_a pack_station_a)
(detach-temperature_profile shipment_a cold_chain_profile)
(reopen-release_window shipment_a cutoff_window_a)
(assign-source_location shipment_b warehouse_a)
(attach-release_window shipment_b cutoff_window_a)
(authorize-processing_release shipment_b cutoff_window_a)
(attach-temperature_profile shipment_b cold_chain_profile)
(select-carrier_preference shipment_b carrier_preference_b cutoff_window_a)
(allocate-handling_resource shipment_b pack_station_a)
(prepare-carrier_booking shipment_b pack_station_a)
(confirm-carrier_manifest shipment_b cold_chain_profile)
(commit-dispatch_clearance shipment_b)
(commit-outbound_release_with_service_level shipment_b express_service_level cutoff_window_a)
(close-shipment_outbound shipment_b warehouse_a express_service_level)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a planning domain for cut-off sensitive order release with source and destination fulfillment units, shipments, etc. The goal is to have outbound_closed for source_fulfillment_unit_a, destination_fulfillment_unit_a, shipment_a, and shipment_b.


## Understanding the planning domain
- The problem focuses on a cut-off sensitive order release system with source and destination fulfillment units.
- It involves managing shipments, specifically shipment_a and shipment_b, within this system.

## Defining the goal
- The objective is to achieve an "outbound_closed" status for source_fulfillment_unit_a and destination_fulfillment_unit_a.
- This status must also be achieved for both shipment_a and shipment_b to complete the planning process.

## Identifying entity relationships  
- All entities—source_fulfillment_unit_a, destination_fulfillment_unit_a, shipment_a, and shipment_b—are classified as customer_orders.  
- Source and destination units are order_segments linked to customer_orders, while shipments belong to shipment_group under customer_orders.

## Exploring closure process  
- Closing each entity requires processing as a customer_order, following steps like registering, assigning locations, and linking release windows.  
- Actions such as authorizing release and allocating handling depend on preconditions like processing_release_approved, which needs order_intake_logged and source_location_selected.  

## Examining resource constraints  
- Shared resources like transport_unit_a and pack_station_a, plus only one fulfillment_item_a, may limit concurrent processing.  
- Initial conditions show no assigned source_location, requiring registration to start the closure process.

## Examining resource constraints  
- Shared resources include cutoff_window_a, pack_station_a, fulfillment_item_a, and transport_unit_a, limiting concurrent processing.  
- Pack_unit_a is dedicated to shipment_a, while pack_unit_b is for shipment_b, potentially simplifying resource allocation for shipments.  

## Analyzing process steps  
- Each customer_order (source_unit, dest_unit, shipment_a, shipment_b) must follow a pipeline to reach outbound_closed, starting with registration.  
- Steps include assigning source location, attaching release window, and authorizing release, each with specific preconditions like logged status and resource availability.  

## Exploring unit activation  
- Source units can be activated via open_source_segment or reserve_source_item, both requiring approved status and linked resources.  
- Actions like activate_source_unit and finalize_source_segment further prepare units, depending on reserved items and readiness status.  

## Planning resource management  
- Reserve_transport_unit_for_segments links activated source and destination units with transport, requiring open segments and available transport units.  
- Free_ and detach actions can release resources for reuse, potentially enabling sequential processing of multiple customer_orders.

## Assessing Resource Sharing  
- With four customer orders (co) and limited resources like one cutoff_window_a, resources must be reused across orders.  
- Actions like reopen_release_window allow detaching and reattaching release windows, enabling sequential processing to manage sharing.  

## Planning Sequential Processing  
- Processing source_fulfillment_unit_a (sfua) and dest_fulfillment_unit_a (dfua) first is necessary to prepare for shipments, given shared resources.  
- Handling resources like pack_station_a can only be linked to one unit at a time, requiring sequential freeing and allocation.  

## Outlining Unit Activation Steps  
- For sfua, steps include registering, assigning warehouse_a, attaching cutoff_window_a, and authorizing release to get approved status.  
- Activation can then proceed via open_source_segment and activate_source_unit, using pack_station_a, without needing fulfillment_item_a.  

## Exploring Destination Unit Readiness  
- Similarly, dfua requires opening destination_segment and activating with pack_station_a, but resource linkage (e.g., handling resource) conflicts with sfua.  
- Both units need linked release windows and handling resources, highlighting the need for sequential processing to free up shared resources.  

## Evaluating Transport Integration  
- Reserve_transport_unit_for_segments needs both sfua and dfua activated and ready, with open segments and linked release windows.  
- Staging transport units further depends on release window availability, reinforcing the necessity of sequential resource management.

## Analyzing Unit Closure  
- Closing source_fulfillment_unit requires it to be linked to a source_location, with no shipment involvement in the release process.  
- Similarly, closing destination_fulfillment_unit needs source_location_linked and service_level_linked, ensuring resource availability post-closure.  

## Reviewing Resource Constraints  
- Limited resources include one release_window, one handling_resource, three source_locations, one service_level_profile, two carrier_preferences, and one temperature_profile.  
- Carrier_preferences and temperature_profile can be detached to free up, but no action exists to unreserve transport_units once linked.  

## Planning Resource Sequencing  
- Processing units sequentially is necessary due to shared resources like handling_resource and release_window, requiring detachment and reallocation.  
- Both source and destination units share the same shipment_segments, needing activation before reserving the single transport_unit.  

## Exploring Temperature and Carrier Needs  
- Units don’t require temperature_profiles, but shipments need them for carrier manifest confirmation, using detach_temperature_profile to free resources.  
- Carrier_preferences, initially available for two shipments, cannot be detached once selected, limiting flexibility in resource use.

## Continuing Resource Sequencing  
- After closing sfua, the release_window remains linked, requiring reopening to free it for dfua use.  
- Reopen release_window for sfua first, then attach it to dfua to proceed with commitment and closure, ensuring sequential resource management.  

## Processing Shipment Steps  
- Register and process shipment_a by assigning warehouse_c, linking release_window, and authorizing processing to approve.  
- Attach temperature_profile and select carrier_preference for shipment_a, ensuring resources like cold_chain_profile and carrier_preference_a are linked and unavailable afterward.  

## Confirming Shipment Readiness  
- Allocate handling_resource and prepare carrier_booking for shipment_a, requiring prior approval and available resources.  
- Confirm carrier_manifest using cold_chain_profile, marking shipment_a ready without needing unit readiness for carrier path.

## Managing Unit Activation  
- Activated source_fulfillment_unit_a with source_shipment_segment_a and pack_station_a, ensuring resources are ready.  
- Freed handling_resource and reopened release_window for source_fulfillment_unit_a, making segments open and resources available.  

## Handling Destination Unit Setup  
- Assigned warehouse_b to destination_fulfillment_unit_a and attached release_window, authorizing processing and allocating handling_resource.  
- Activated destination_fulfillment_unit_a with destination_shipment_segment_a, freeing resources and reopening release_window afterward.  

## Reserving Transport Unit  
- Reserved transport_unit_a for both source and destination segments, linking it to ensure coordination.  
- Staged transport_unit_a with source_fulfillment_unit_a, requiring release_window attachment for readiness.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
